items.reason: The PR replaces the old log_rate_limit guide with the new request/access/diagnostic sample-rate model, propagation behavior, derivation rules, and configuration examples.
The PR removes DATASYSTEM_LOG_RATE_LIMIT from the client environment guide, matching the code removal of that client-side env override.
The PR replaces the dscli log_rate_limit option with request_sample_rate, access_sample_rate, diagnostic_sample_rate, and documents derivation semantics.
The PR adds a source-backed design for LogSampler covering the new random sampling categories, config propagation, hot-path constraints, and removal of log_rate_limit compatibility.
The PR routes logging-context readers to the new LogSampler design and updates the module responsibilities and component table for unified sampling.
This existing logging design doc still lists log_rate_limit, DATASYSTEM_LOG_RATE_LIMIT, LogRateLimiter, and old register-sync behavior, but the PR removes that model.
This trace doc still describes local sampling decisions under log_rate_limit and a LogRateLimiter per-second counter instead of the new LogSampler trace-state behavior.
This PLOG plan still references log_rate_limit and LogRateLimiter::ShouldLog even though PLOG is now handled as diagnostic sampling under LogSampler.
This implementation summary still says PLOG bypasses log_rate_limit request sampling and was not updated for the new diagnostic_sample_rate behavior.
